blocks:
سُورة القيامة

Meccan, and it consists of forty verses according to the Kūfan count, and thirty-nine in the remaining counts. The point of difference is the verse لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِ ** (75:16), which is counted by the Kūfan school. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) لَا أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ ** (1) I swear by the Day of Resurrection, The particle لَا (lā) is extra (زائدة) for emphasis (للتأكيد).

(٢) وَلَا أُقْسِمُ بِالنَّفْسِ اللَّوَّامَةِ ** (2) and I swear by the self-reproaching soul! which constantly reproaches itself, even if it strives diligently in obedience (الطاعة).

(٣) أَيَحْسَبُ الْإِنْسَانُ أَلَّنْ نَجْمَعَ عِظَامَهُ ** (3) Does man suppose that We will not put together his bones? after their dispersion (تفرّقها). It is said: It was revealed concerning ʿAdī ibn Rabīʿah, who asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about the matter of the Resurrection. So, He informed him about it, and he said: "Even if I were to witness that Day, I would not believe you, or [until] Allah gathers these bones."

(٤) بَلى ** نجمعها قادِرِينَ عَلى أَنْ نُسَوِّيَ بَنانَهُ (4) Yes, We are able to set his fingertips right. (We gather them) by assembling his phalanges (سلاميّاته) and joining some of them to others, just as they were, despite their smallness and delicacy. So how (can He not gather) the large bones? Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The tips of the fingers (أطراف الأصابع); if Allah had willed, He would have made them smooth (لسوّاها). (٥) بَلْ يُرِيدُ الْإِنْسانُ لِيَفْجُرَ أَمامَهُ ** (5) Rather man desires to go on sinning in advance. (He desires) to persist in his wickedness (فجوره) in the time that lies ahead of him. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: He commits the sin (الذنب) first and postpones repentance (التوبة), saying, "I will repent later." (٦) يَسْئَلُ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ الْقِيامَةِ ** (6) He asks, 'When is the Day of Resurrection?' When will it be? (This question is posed out of) deeming it far-fetched (استبعاد) or mockery (استهزاء). (٧) فَإِذا بَرِقَ الْبَصَرُ ** (7) When the sight is dazzled, It becomes bewildered out of terror (فزعاً), derived from the dazzling (برق) of a man when he looks at lightning (البرق) and his sight is stunned (دهش بصره). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The sight dazzles (يبرق البصر) and cannot blink (أن يطرف). And it was read (وقرئ) with a fathah on the rā’ (بفتح الراء), which is a dialect (لغة) or derived from shining (البريق) due to the intensity of its staring (شخوصه). (٨) وَخَسَفَ الْقَمَرُ ** (8) and the moon is eclipsed, Its light departs. (٩) وَجُمِعَ الشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ ** (9) and the sun and the moon are brought together, In al-Ghaybah, narrated from al-Qa'im (peace be upon him): He was asked when this matter (هذا الأمر) would occur. He said: "When a barrier is placed between you and the path to the Ka'bah (سبيل الكعبة), and the sun and the moon are brought together, and the planets (الكواكب) and the stars (النجوم) revolve around them." It was asked: "When?" He replied: "In such and such a year, the Beast of the Earth (دابّة الأرض) will emerge from between al-Safa and al-Marwah, having with Him the staff of Moses (عصا موسى) and the ring of Solomon (خاتم سليمان) (peace be upon him), driving the people toward the Gathering (المحشر)." And it was said that what is meant by these verses is the appearance of the signs of death (أمارات الموت).

(١٠) ** يَقُولُ الْإِنْسانُ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَيْنَ الْمَفَرُّ ** (10) On that day man will say, 'Where is the escape?' He says it with the utterance of one despairing of finding it, yet wishing for it. (١١) ** كَلَّا ** (11) No indeed! A deterrent against seeking escape. لا وَزَرَ ** (There is) no refuge. There is no shelter (ملجأ). It is borrowed from the mountain (الجبل), and its derivation is from الوزر (burden), which is the heaviness (الثّقل). (١٢) ** إِلى رَبِّكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْمُسْتَقَرُّ ** (12) On that day the destination will be toward your Lord. To Him alone, and to His judgment and His will, is the place of flight (or escape). (١٣) ** يُنَبَّؤُا الْإِنْسانُ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِما قَدَّمَ وَأَخَّرَ ** (13) On that day man will be informed of what he has sent ahead and what he has left behind. Al-Qummi said: He will be informed of what he has sent ahead and what he has left behind. And narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding: بِما قَدَّمَ ** (of what he has sent ahead): [It refers to] good and evil. And وَأَخَّرَ ** (and what he has left behind): It is whatever tradition (سنّة) he established for those after him to follow. If it was evil, he will bear a burden (وزر) like their burdens, and nothing will be diminished from their burdens. And if it was good, he will receive a reward (أجور) like their rewards, and nothing will be diminished from their rewards.

(١٤) بَلِ الْإِنْسانُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ بَصِيرَةٌ ** (14) Nay, man is a witness against his own soul, A clear proof against its deeds, because he is a witness to them, or a seeing eye concerning them, so he does not need to be informed. (١٥) وَلَوْ أَلْقى مَعاذِيرَهُ ** (15) even though he offers his excuses. Even if he comes with everything that he can use to excuse himself. Al-Qummi said: He knows what he has done, even if he offers an excuse.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: What does one of you gain by displaying a good deed and concealing something (bad)? Does he not know, when he refers back to his soul, that it is not so? And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: ** بَلِ الْإِنْسانُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ بَصِيرَةٌ (Nay, man is a witness against his own soul). Indeed, if the inner self (السريرة) is rectified, the outer self (العلانية) is strengthened.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He recited this Ayah and said: What does man gain by offering excuses to people contrary to what Allah knows about him? Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to say: "Whoever conceals an inner self (سريرة), Allah will clothe Him with its cloak (رداءها). If it is good, then good; and if it is evil, then evil."

** (١٦) لا تُحَرِّكْ بِهِ لِسانَكَ لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِ ** (16) Do not move your tongue with it to hasten it. Do not move, O Muhammad, your tongue with the Quran before its revelation is completed, so that you take it hastily, fearing that it might escape you. In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, when the Quran was revealed to him, would hasten to move his tongue due to his love for it and his eagerness to receive it and memorize it, fearing that he might forget it. So Allah forbade Him from that. And another perspective regarding its cause of revelation, narrated from al-Qummi, will come shortly.

(١٧) إِنَّ عَلَيْنا جَمْعَهُ ** (17) Indeed, it is up to Us to put it together in your chest, وَقُرْآنَهُ ** and establishing its recitation upon your tongue. And this is the reason (تعليل) for the prohibition. (١٨) فَإِذا قَرَأْناهُ ** (18) So when We recite it, through the tongue of Jibra'il (Gabriel) upon you, فَاتَّبِعْ قُرْآنَهُ ** its recitation by repeating it until it is established in your mind. In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn 'Abbas: So the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم after this, when Jibra'il (Gabriel) descended upon him, he lowered his head, and when he departed, he recited it.

(١٩) ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَيْنا بَيانَهُ ** (19) then indeed it is up to Us to clarify it. The clarification of what is difficult for you concerning its meanings. (٢٠) كَلَّا ** (20) No indeed! Perhaps it is a deterrent against man offering excuses, despite the fact that he is a witness against his own soul (بَصِيرَة), and what is between them is an interjection (اعتراض). ** بَلْ تُحِبُّونَ الْعاجِلَةَ ** (20) Rather you love the fleeting life, Al-Qummi said: The present world (الدنيا الحاضرة). (٢١) وَتَذَرُونَ الْآخِرَةَ ** (21) and abandon the Hereafter. He said: You abandon (تَدْعُونَ). And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء) in both (verbs). (٢٢) وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ ناضِرَةٌ. (22) Faces on that day will be radiant, (٢٣) إِلى رَبِّها ناظِرَةٌ ** (23) looking at their Lord. He said: They look at the Face of Allah, meaning: at the Mercy of Allah and His favor. And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: It means shining (مُشْرِقَة), awaiting the reward of their Lord. And in al-Tawhid and al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: The friends of Allah (أولياء الله) will arrive, after the reckoning is finished, at a river called al-Hayawan (الحيوان). They will bathe in it and drink from it, and their faces will become white with radiance (اشراقاً), and every impurity (قَذَى) and hardship (وَعْث) will depart from them. Then they will be commanded to enter Paradise. It is from this station (المقام) that they look at their Lord, [observing] how He rewards them. He said: That is the Almighty's saying: إِلى رَبِّها ناظِرَةٌ (looking at their Lord). And what is meant by looking at Him is looking at His reward, may He be blessed and exalted. And it was added in al-Ihtijaj: And al-nāẓirah (النّاظرة) in some linguistic usage means the awaiting one (المنتظرة). Have you not heard His saying: فَناظِرَةٌ بِمَ يَرْجِعُ الْمُرْسَلُونَ (awaiting what the envoys bring back)? meaning: awaiting (منتظرة).

(٢٤) ** وَوُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ باسِرَةٌ ** (24) and [other] faces on that day will be scowling, Intensely frowning. (٢٥) ** تَظُنُّ أَنْ يُفْعَلَ بِها فاقِرَةٌ ** (25) knowing that a calamity will befall them. A disaster that breaks the vertebrae (الفقار). (٢٦) ** كَلَّا ** A deterrent against preferring the world over the Hereafter ** إِذا بَلَغَتِ التَّراقِيَ ** Al-Qummi said: It means the soul (النّفس) when it reaches the collarbone (الترقوة). (٢٧) ** وَقِيلَ مَنْ راقٍ ** (27) and it is said, ‘Who will exorcise [him]?’ He said: It is said to him, "Who will cure (يرقيك) you?" (٢٨) ** وَظَنَّ أَنَّهُ الْفِراقُ ** (28) and he knows that it is the parting, He knew that what had descended upon him was the parting from the world and its beloved things. (٢٩) ** وَالْتَفَّتِ السَّاقُ بِالسَّاقِ ** (29) and leg is entwined with leg, The severity of parting from the world was entwined with the severity of the fear of the Hereafter. (٣٠) ** إِلى رَبِّكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْمَساقُ ** (30) toward your Lord will be the driving on that day. Al-Qummi said: They will be driven toward Allah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "That is the son of Adam when death descends upon him. He says: 'Is there any physician?' It is the parting (الْفِراقُ). He becomes certain of separating from his beloved ones." He (peace be upon him) said: ** وَالْتَفَّتِ السَّاقُ بِالسَّاقِ ** (and leg is entwined with leg) means the world (الدنيا) is entwined with the Hereafter (الآخرة). ** إِلى رَبِّكَ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْمَساقُ ** (toward your Lord will be the driving on that day). He said: "The destination (المصير) is to the Lord of the worlds (ربّ العالمين)."

(٣١) فَلا صَدَّقَ ** (So he neither confirmed (the truth) nor did he pray) what must be confirmed ** وَلا صَلَّى ** what was obligated upon him. (٣٢) وَلكِنْ كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّى ** (but he denied and turned away) from obedience (الطاعة). (٣٣) ثُمَّ ذَهَبَ إِلى أَهْلِهِ يَتَمَطَّى ** (Then he went swaggering to his family) meaning, he strutted (يتبختر) in pride (افتخاراً) because of that, derived from al-matt (المط). (٣٤) أَوْلى لَكَ فَأَوْلى ** (Woe to you, and woe to you!) It is said this means: Woe (ويل) to you. (٣٥) ثُمَّ أَوْلى لَكَ فَأَوْلى ** (Again, woe to you, and woe to you!) meaning, that will be repeated upon you time after time (مرّة بعد أخرى).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: "A distance for you from the good of this world, and a distance for you from the good of the Hereafter." Al-Qummi said: The reason for its revelation was that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called for the allegiance (بيعة) to Ali (peace be upon him) on the Day of Ghadir Khumm. When He reached the people and informed them concerning Ali (peace be upon him) what He intended to inform, the people returned. Mu'awiyah leaned upon al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah and Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, then he approached, swaggering toward his family, saying: "We will never acknowledge the Wilayah (الولاية) of Ali, nor will we confirm the statement (مقالته) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." So, Allah, majestic is His remembrance, revealed: فَلا صَدَّقَ وَلا صَلَّى ** [the rest of] the Ayat. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit, intending to disavow him, but Allah revealed: لا تُحَرِّكْ بِهِ لِسانَكَ لِتَعْجَلَ بِهِ ** (Do not move your tongue with it to hasten it). So, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله الله عليه وآله وسلم remained silent and did not name him.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He took the hand of Abu Jahl, then said to him: أَوْلى لَكَ فَأَوْلى ثُمَّ أَوْلى لَكَ فَأَوْلى (Woe to you, and woe to you! Again, woe to you, and woe to you!) Abu Jahl said: "With what are you threatening me? Neither you nor your Lord can do anything to me! Indeed, I am the most powerful (أعزّ) person in this valley." So, Allah, the Glorified, revealed [the Ayah] just as His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had said to him.

** أَيَحْسَبُ الْإِنْسانُ أَنْ يُتْرَكَ سُدىً ** (36) Does man suppose that he will be left without a purpose? neglected (مهملاً). Al-Qummi said: He will not be reckoned with, nor punished, nor questioned about anything.

** أَلَمْ يَكُ نُطْفَةً مِنْ مَنِيٍّ يُمْنى.** (37) Was he not a drop of semen that was emitted?

** ثُمَّ كانَ عَلَقَةً فَخَلَقَ فَسَوَّى ** (38) Then he was a clinging mass. Then He created and fashioned [him], so He proportioned him (فقدّره) and made him balanced (فعدله).

** فَجَعَلَ مِنْهُ الزَّوْجَيْنِ ** (39) and made of him two kinds: the two types (الصنفين) الذَّكَرَ وَالْأُنْثى. the male and the female.

** أَلَيْسَ ذلِكَ بِقادِرٍ عَلى أَنْ يُحْيِيَ الْمَوْتى.** (40) Is not such [a one] able to revive the dead?

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: When this verse was revealed, He said: "Glory be to You, O Allah, yes (بلى)."

He said: And this is what is narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them). And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): When He reads this Surah, He says that (ذلك) upon finishing it.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Whoever persists in reciting لا أقسم (I swear) and acts upon it, Allah will raise him from his grave with His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in the most beautiful form, and He will give him glad tidings and smile in his face until he passes over the Sirat (Bridge) and the Balance (al-Mizan).
